overview: This image shows White-tailed Kites ( Elanus leucurus ) engaged in a food exchange, a behavior commonly seen during mating or when feeding young. These raptors are identified by their striking white plumage, grey wings, black shoulder patches, and piercing red eyes. They are known for their distinct hunting style of hovering over open grasslands to spot prey like mice before diving.

This image shows White-tailed Kites ( Elanus leucurus ) engaged in a food exchange, a behavior commonly seen during mating or when feeding young.
These raptors are identified by their striking white plumage, grey wings, black shoulder patches, and piercing red eyes.
They are known for their distinct hunting style of hovering over open grasslands to spot prey like mice before diving.
